summary: Well-executed implementation of Apache Spark 4.1 support with excellent architectural discipline. However, critical test coverage gaps and version management issues must be addressed. The approach properly handles SPARK-52787 package reorganization with minimal code duplication and maintains backward compatibility.

findings.title: Missing Critical Test Coverage
findings.description: The serialize() and deserialize() methods that extend HDFSMetadataLog[String] lack direct unit tests. Package reorganization could introduce subtle serialization behavior differences between Spark 3 and 4.1, potentially breaking checkpoint compatibility.
findings.suggestion: Add unit tests covering serialization round-trip, backward compatibility with existing checkpoint data, and malformed input handling.

findings.title: Parent Module Version Mismatch
findings.description: New module inherits from azure-cosmos-spark_3 version 0.0.1-beta.1 but has its own version 4.47.0. Creates confusion in version inheritance chain and potential build issues.
findings.suggestion: Consider creating a common parent module or document the intentional version divergence clearly.

findings.title: Inlined Validation Logic Increases Maintenance Burden
findings.description: The validateVersion method duplicates logic from Spark's MetadataVersionUtil to avoid runtime dependency issues. Creates technical debt and maintenance overhead when Spark updates its validation logic.
findings.suggestion: Document this as technical debt with a plan to consolidate when older Spark versions are deprecated, or create a thin abstraction layer.

findings.title: Missing Integration Test for Package Reorganization
findings.description: No dedicated test validates that SPARK-52787 fix works in real Spark 4.1 environment. Runtime failures could occur if class loading fails in actual Spark 4.1 clusters.
findings.suggestion: Create Spark41PackageReorganizationITest.scala that instantiates classes using new package paths and verifies no ClassNotFoundException occurs.

findings.title: Documentation References Need Update
findings.description: Documentation references 'Spark 3 documentation' but these links should be verified for Spark 4.1 relevance. Could mislead users about applicable documentation.
findings.suggestion: Update note to clarify which aspects of Spark 3 documentation apply to Spark 4.1, or create Spark 4.1-specific documentation.

findings.title: Excellent Source Sharing Architecture
findings.description: The Maven resource copying strategy minimizes code duplication while maintaining clear separation of version-specific concerns.
findings.suggestion: Consider documenting this pattern as a reusable template for future version-specific modules.

findings.title: Proper Infrastructure Integration
findings.description: Version management, CI configuration, and build profiles are correctly updated across all infrastructure components.
findings.suggestion: Maintain this pattern for future upstream dependency upgrades.

findings.title: Backward Compatibility Preserved
findings.description: Documentation clearly states existing checkpoints, offsets, and APIs remain fully compatible.
findings.suggestion: Continue emphasizing backward compatibility guarantees in release notes.
